lines.english: Wonderful is the earth, wonderful the sources of creation.
Wonderful is fire, which works wonders.
Wonderful is the wind, wonderful is the water.
Wonderful is union, and wonderful is separation.
Wonderful are the tastes to which mortals are attached.
Wonderful is hunger, wonderful is satisfaction.
Wonderful is His Praise, wonderful is His adoration.
Wonderful is closeness, wonderful is distance.
Wonderful is the wilderness, wonderful is the path.
How wonderful to behold the Lord, ever-present here.
O Nanak, those who understand this are blessed with perfect destiny. ||1||
Beholding His wonders, I am wonder-struck.
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਦਿਸੈ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਸੁਣੀਐ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਭਉ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat disai kudarat suneeai kudarat bhau sukh saar |
lines.english: By His Power we see, by His Power we hear; by His Power we have fear, and the essence of happiness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਪਾਤਾਲੀ ਆਕਾਸੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਸਰਬ ਆਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat paataalee aakaasee kudarat sarab aakaar |
lines.english: By His Power the nether worlds exist, and the Akaashic ethers; by His Power the entire creation exists.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਖਾਣਾ ਪੀਣਾ ਪੈਨੑਣੁ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਸਰਬ ਪਿਆਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat khaanaa peenaa painan kudarat sarab piaar |
lines.english: By His Power we eat, drink and dress; by His Power all love exists.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਵੇਦ ਪੁਰਾਣ ਕਤੇਬਾ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਸਰਬ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat ved puraan katebaa kudarat sarab veechaar |
lines.english: By His Power the Vedas and the Puraanas exist, and the Holy Scriptures of the Jewish, Christian and Islamic religions. By His Power all deliberations exist.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਜਾਤੀ ਜਿਨਸੀ ਰੰਗੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਜੀਅ ਜਹਾਨ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat jaatee jinasee rangee kudarat jeea jahaan |
lines.english: - By His Power come the species of all kinds and colors; by His Power the living beings of the world exist.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਨੇਕੀਆ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਬਦੀਆ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਮਾਨੁ ਅਭਿਮਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat nekeea kudarat badeea kudarat maan abhimaan |
lines.english: By His Power virtues exist, and by His Power vices exist. By His Power come honor and dishonor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਪਉਣੁ ਪਾਣੀ ਬੈਸੰਤਰੁ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਧਰਤੀ ਖਾਕੁ ॥
lines.translit: kudarat paun paanee baisantar kudarat dharatee khaak |
lines.english: By His Power wind, water and fire exist; by His Power earth and dust exist.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਤੇਰੀ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਤੂੰ ਕਾਦਿਰੁ ਕਰਤਾ ਪਾਕੀ ਨਾਈ ਪਾਕੁ ॥
lines.translit: sabh teree kudarat toon kaadir karataa paakee naaee paak |
lines.english: Everything is in Your Power, Lord; You are the all-powerful Creator. Your Name is the Holiest of the Holy.
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹੁਕਮੈ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵੇਖੈ ਵਰਤੈ ਤਾਕੋ ਤਾਕੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak hukamai andar vekhai varatai taako taak |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, through the Command of His Will, He beholds and pervades the creation; He is absolutely unrivalled. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੀਨੑੈ ਭੋਗ ਭੋਗਿ ਕੈ ਹੋਇ ਭਸਮੜਿ ਭਉਰੁ ਸਿਧਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: aapeenai bhog bhog kai hoe bhasamarr bhaur sidhaaeaa |
lines.english: Enjoying his pleasures, one is reduced to a pile of ashes, and the soul passes away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਕਰਣੀ ਕੀਰਤਿ ਵਾਚੀਐ ਬਹਿ ਲੇਖਾ ਕਰਿ ਸਮਝਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: agai karanee keerat vaacheeai beh lekhaa kar samajhaaeaa |
lines.english: There, his good and bad deeds are added up; sitting there, his account is read.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਡਾ ਹੋਆ ਦੁਨੀਦਾਰੁ ਗਲਿ ਸੰਗਲੁ ਘਤਿ ਚਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: vaddaa hoaa duneedaar gal sangal ghat chalaaeaa |
lines.english: He may be great, but when he dies, the chain is thrown around his neck, and he is led away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਥਾਉ ਨ ਹੋਵੀ ਪਉਦੀਈ ਹੁਣਿ ਸੁਣੀਐ ਕਿਆ ਰੂਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: thaau na hovee paudeeee hun suneeai kiaa rooaaeaa |
lines.english: He is whipped, but finds no place of rest, and no one hears his cries of pain.
Salok, First Mehl:
In the Fear of God, the wind and breezes ever blow.
In the Fear of God, thousands of rivers flow.
The blind man has wasted his life away. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਇੰਦੁ ਫਿਰੈ ਸਿਰ ਭਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: bhai vich ind firai sir bhaar |
lines.english: In the Fear of God, the clouds move across the sky.
In the Fear of God, the earth is crushed under its burden.
In the Fear of God, fire is forced to labor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਭੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਚੰਦੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhai vich sooraj bhai vich chand |
lines.english: In the Fear of God, the sun shines, and in the Fear of God, the moon reflects.
They travel millions of miles, endlessly.
In the Fear of God, the Righteous Judge of Dharma stands at His Door.
In the Fear of God, multitudes come and go.
In the Fear of God, the warriors and the most powerful heroes exist.
In the Fear of God, the Akaashic ethers are stretched across the sky.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਸਿਧ ਬੁਧ ਸੁਰ ਨਾਥ ॥
lines.translit: bhai vich sidh budh sur naath |
lines.english: In the Fear of God, the Siddhas exist, as do the Buddhas, the demi-gods and Yogis.
O Nanak, the Fearless Lord, the Formless Lord, the True Lord, is One. ||1||
God has inscribed the Inscription of His Fear upon the heads of all.
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਤੇ ਨਚਹਿ ਮੰਗਤੇ ਗਿੜਿ ਮੁੜਿ ਪੂਰਹਿ ਤਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: kete nacheh mangate girr murr pooreh taal |
lines.english: So many beggars dance, spinning around to the beat.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਤੀਆ ਕੰਨੑ ਕਹਾਣੀਆ ਕੇਤੇ ਬੇਦ ਬੀਚਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: keteea kana kahaaneea kete bed beechaar |
lines.english: There are so many stories of Krishna, so many who reflect over the Vedas.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੁ ਹੋਰਿ ਕੇਤੇ ਰਾਮ ਰਵਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: naanak nirbhau nirankaar hor kete raam ravaal |
lines.english: O Nanak, the Lord is fearless and formless; myriads of others, like Rama, are mere dust before Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਜਾਰੀ ਬਾਜਾਰ ਮਹਿ ਆਇ ਕਢਹਿ ਬਾਜਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: baajaaree baajaar meh aae kadteh baajaar |
lines.english: The magicians perform their magic in the market place, creating a false illusion.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਾਵਹਿ ਰਾਜੇ ਰਾਣੀਆ ਬੋਲਹਿ ਆਲ ਪਤਾਲ ॥
lines.translit: gaaveh raaje raaneea boleh aal pataal |
lines.english: They sing as kings and queens, and speak of this and that.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਟਕਿਆ ਕੇ ਮੁੰਦੜੇ ਲਖ ਟਕਿਆ ਕੇ ਹਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: lakh ttakiaa ke mundarre lakh ttakiaa ke haar |
lines.english: They wear earrings, and necklaces worth thousands of dollars.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤੁ ਤਨਿ ਪਾਈਅਹਿ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਸੇ ਤਨ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਛਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jit tan paaeeeh naanakaa se tan hoveh chhaar |
lines.english: Those bodies on which they are worn, O Nanak, those bodies turn to ashes.
